questions.question: What are the three sub-problems of music generation?
options: Instrumental generation (text → audio, MusicGen/Stable Audio), full song generation with vocals + lyrics (Suno, Udio, ACE-Step), and conditional/controllable generation (extend, inpaint a bridge, swap genre, stem-separate)
MIDI, sheet music, and tablature export
questions.explanation: By 2026 the technical problem is mostly solved — the legal problem (Warner's $500M Suno settlement, UMG's Udio settlement) is what reshaped the field.

questions.question: How does MusicGen's token-LM approach work?
options: Condition on text/melody embeddings and autoregressively predict EnCodec tokens (32 kHz, 4 codebooks), then decode with EnCodec — a strong MIT-licensed baseline that struggles past 30 seconds
It samples from a MIDI database
It splices loops from a licensed library
questions.explanation: The codec-token LM is one of two open families. ACE-Step (4B, April 2026, Apache-2.0) extends the pattern to full-song lyric-conditioned generation — the open community's closest thing to Suno.

questions.question: Where does latent diffusion (Stable Audio, AudioLDM) excel and fall short?
options: Excels at loops, sound design, and ambient textures; not great at structured full songs — while production systems (Suno v5, Udio v4) are likely hybrids of an AR codec LM with diffusion-based decoding
It beats every token-LM at all lengths
questions.explanation: The two open families split by structure: token LMs hold musical structure better over time; diffusion gives texture quality. Udio v4's inpainting + stem separation is the 2026 feature bar.

questions.question: How is generated music evaluated?
options: FAD (Fréchet Audio Distance over VGGish/PANNs embeddings, lower better — SOTA ~3.0 on MusicCaps), CLAP score for text-audio alignment, human ELO for musicality, plus artifact checks like off-beat transitions and structure loss past 30s
questions.explanation: Suno v5's ELO 1293 leads subjective quality. The characteristic AR failure mode — vocal-phrase drift and structure loss past 30 seconds — is why length columns matter in the model map.

questions.question: What did the 2025-2026 legal settlements change for music AI?
options: Warner's $500M settlement with Suno (and UMG's with Udio) gave labels oversight of AI likeness, music rights, and user-generated tracks — licensing, not architecture, became the moat
questions.explanation: The earthquake reshaped who can train on what: open models (ACE-Step, YuE under Apache-2.0) compete on permissively-licensed data while commercial leaders operate under label agreements.
